task.title: Handle `~~~` and arbitrary length code fences

rating.rationale: Agent fully completed the task with zero CEO intervention. The state-machine approach replacing the regex is sound and handles tilde fences, arbitrary-length fences, and nested fences correctly. Communication is excellent — clear problem statement, approach rationale, code walkthrough, and test coverage table. Minor deduction: the `fmt:off/on` directive handling wasn't part of the original issue scope (feature creep), and 7 tests is decent but could include edge cases like mismatched fence types (opening with tildes, attempting close with backticks).
